title: VerifyCassettesEnvelope
description: Structured stdout of `cowork-harness verify-cassettes --output-format json` (SPEC §11.1) — the token-free CI gate over committed cassettes (privacy scan + staleness). A covered surface of the 1.0 compatibility contract (SPEC §12): parse this, not the human-readable text output.

version.description: cowork-harness package version that produced this envelope.

error.description: null on success (verify-cassettes reports per-cassette failures inside results[], not here); the shared error-envelope object on a top-level usage/runtime failure.

ok.description: false if any real privacy finding (cls != 'unscanned'), staleness message, version mismatch, or unreadable cassette. Mirrors the exit code (0 clean / 1 not-ok).

coverage.description: Which scans actually ran — false under --skip-privacy / --skip-staleness / --skip-scenario-drift. A consumer gating on ok:true should also assert the coverage it expects (a skipped scan can't fail).

results.description: One entry per cassette file, including unreadable ones (tallied via `error`, never a crash that aborts the batch).

findings.description: Privacy-scan findings. cls 'unscanned' (a hash-only >cap artifact body — nothing committed to leak) is reported but does NOT fail the gate; every other class does.

staleness.description: Drift / unresolvable-fingerprint messages — each one fails the gate. Empty under --skip-staleness.

notes.description: NON-failing informational channel (never affects ok / the exit code) — e.g. a pre-effectiveFidelity cassette with an explicit tier: statically knowable, nothing baseline-dependent to verify. Text output renders these as `·` rows.

version.description: Format/version failures: the cassette was written by a NEWER harness than this one understands. Always fails the gate (can't verify ⇒ not green), independent of --skip-staleness.

scenarioDrift.description: Scenario-content (prompt) drift: the committed on-disk scenario's prompt diverged from the cassette's frozen prompt. A definite divergence — each message fails the gate (own bucket so --skip-staleness can't mask it). Empty under --skip-scenario-drift, or when the source isn't resolvable (that becomes a non-failing `notes` entry instead).

error.description: Present when the cassette file itself is malformed/unreadable; the file is tallied as a gate failure and the batch continues.
